start.speaker: gate_guard
start.text: Another traveler. Either you're brave or you haven't heard. Ashvale isn't exactly a destination these days.

options.text: [Show Silver Compass] Do you recognize the name on this? Silas Maren?
I need a place to sleep. Is there an inn?
Just passing through. Let me in.

weather_talk.speaker: gate_guard
weather_talk.text: Always is, this time of year. Autumn used to be warmer, though. Before the... well. Before everything. Used to fish with a friend of mine on evenings like this. Long time ago.

harlan_regret.speaker: gate_guard
harlan_regret.text: Silas? That was... a long time ago. Just someone I knew. Where did you find that?

options.text: Just someone you knew? Your face says otherwise, Harlan.
[Hold compass closer] Look at the inscription. 'For my children.' You were more than acquaintances.
Fair enough. Where can I find a place to sleep?

harlan_regret_real.speaker: gate_guard
harlan_regret_real.text: ...Fine. We were friends. Good friends. Before the Lord called in the debt. Before we all stood by and watched the roots take him. I stood at that gate and I did nothing. That's the kind of man I am.

harlan_regret_2.speaker: gate_guard
harlan_regret_2.text: The well water isn't just poisoned, stranger. It's bitter with the memory of what we allowed to happen. If you're here to settle Silas's score... just be careful. Ashvale doesn't like being reminded of its sins.

explain_situation.speaker: gate_guard
explain_situation.text: Crops are rotting in the fields. Livestock dying. Half the village thinks it's a curse, the other half blames the lord. Me? I think something's wrong with the water. Don't drink from the well — I mean it.

directions_inn.speaker: gate_guard
directions_inn.text: Greta runs the Pig and Whistle — up the main road, can't miss it. She'll have a room if you've got coin. Tell her Harlan sent you, might save you a copper.

warning.speaker: gate_guard
warning.text: One more thing, stranger. Don't drink the well water. And don't go near Thornwood after dark. Whatever's killing the crops... I've heard things moving in those trees at night. Things that don't sound like animals.
